stages.title: Identity and access
stages.description: Confirm the protected browser path works and review the governed access expectations before making changes.

items.title: Confirm the protected ops portal path
items.description: Use the authenticated shell as your default browser-first entry so the rest of the checklist points at live platform surfaces.

items.title: Review the operator onboarding contract
items.description: Refresh the current SSO, TOTP, SSH, and roster-backed onboarding expectations before you operate on behalf of another human.

stages.title: Orientation
stages.description: Learn the shared shell, where status lives, and which browser surface should be your next stop.

items.title: Review the portal operating model
items.description: Understand how the interactive portal, launcher, and linked product-native tools fit together before you branch into deeper admin work.

items.title: Inspect runtime assurance and live health
items.description: Use the shared shell to see how the platform communicates health, degraded state, and next actions.

stages.title: Safe first task
stages.description: Start with a read-only or reversible operator action so you can succeed before you unlock higher-risk controls.

items.title: Run the validation-gate status workflow
items.description: The shared runbook launcher provides a low-risk first task that stays inside the governed browser path and returns structured status.

stages.title: Collaboration and alerts
stages.description: Know where drift, recent live applies, and operational follow-up items surface before you touch the higher-risk admin lanes.

items.title: Review drift and notifications
items.description: See how the shell presents unsuppressed reconciliation pressure and maintenance context.

items.title: Review recent live applies and unreleased notes
items.description: Use the changelog panel to understand what changed recently before you operate on production systems.

stages.title: Advanced capability unlock
stages.description: Administrator launcher destinations, deploy/restart/secret actions, and mutating runbooks stay hidden or disabled until the required early stages are complete or you explicitly reveal them for this session.
